message:
frankenjax-dynus1 vectorize batch dynamic_update_slice with static starts

Mirrors the previous batch_dynamic_slice fast path on the
DynamicUpdateSlice side so vmap-compiled writes also vectorize when
the start indices are static. The DynamicSlice path read a batched
operand at static offsets and returned a batched output; the
matching update operation reads a batched operand AND a batched
update tensor at static offsets and returns a batched output where
the update has been spliced in along the leading batch axis.

batch_dynamic_update_slice_static_starts handles the case where:

* operand and update are both batched (different operands but the
  same batch axis after move_batch_dim_to_front normalizes both
  to position 0).
* All start indices are unbatched i64 scalars.
* operand and update have matching rank, the rank matches
  inputs.len() - 1 after the leading axis, and their first
  (batch) dimension is the same size — so the splice can rewrite
  index 0 of starts to 0 and apply the same logical splice across
  every batch element.

When all preconditions hold, the rule prepends a 0 start index
(targeting the new leading batch dim), forwards the original
slice_sizes-equivalent update tensor, and re-issues
DynamicUpdateSlice with the rewritten start vector. The result is
re-tagged with batch_dim=0 so downstream batching consumers see
the canonical "batched on the leading axis" form. Returns Ok(None)
on any precondition mismatch and falls through to
batch_passthrough_leading which keeps the per-element semantics for
mixed-batched starts and unbatched operands.

Three new test cases:

* test_batch_trace_dynamic_update_slice_static_start_direct_1d
  — 2x5 batched operand, 2x2 batched update, single static start=2.
* test_batch_trace_dynamic_update_slice_static_start_direct_2d
  — 2x3x4 batched operand, 2x2x2 batched update, two static starts.
* test_batch_trace_dynamic_update_slice_static_start_moves_nonleading_batch
  — proves batch_dim=1 inputs are rotated to leading via
  move_batch_dim_to_front before the splice (covered by the rest
  of the diff in the matching test block).
